claims.claim: In LFP batteries undergoing thermal runaway in a nitrogen atmosphere, H2 proportion surges from 42.9% at 25% SOC to 73.3% at 100% SOC, while CO2 proportion decreases markedly from 50.4% at 25% SOC to 16.3% at 100% SOC.
claims.quote: the proportion of the primary fire-retardant component, CO2, decreases markedly from 50.4% at 25% SOC to 16.3% at 100% SOC... The main combustible component, H2, surges from 42.9% at 25% SOC to 73.3% at 100% SOC.

claims.claim: Explosion severity of LFP battery thermal runaway gases mixed with methane peaks at methane concentrations between 4% and 6%, with a maximum overpressure of approximately 0.62 MPa (at 75% SOC and 5% methane) and a maximum pressure rise rate of approximately 8.8 MPa/s.
claims.quote: Explosion severity peaked at methane concentrations between 4% and 6%, with a maximum overpressure of approximately 0.62 MPa and a maximum pressure rise rate of approximately 8.8 MPa/s.

claims.claim: When methane concentration exceeds 8%, oxygen deficiency causes incomplete combustion, resulting in a sharp increase in carbon monoxide and a reduction in carbon dioxide in the post-explosion exhaust, markedly increasing toxicity and secondary hazards.
claims.quote: When methane exceeded 8%, oxygen deficiency caused incomplete combustion, resulting in a sharp increase in carbon monoxide and a reduction in carbon dioxide, which markedly increased toxicity and secondary hazards.

claims.claim: Peak free radical concentration (sum of H, O, and OH molar fractions) increases with methane concentration, reaching a maximum at 8–9% methane before declining, and does not align with peak explosion overpressure, revealing a temporal decoupling between radical activity and macroscopic overpressure.
claims.quote: the peak concentration of free radicals increases significantly with CH4 concentration, reaching a maximum at 8–9% before declining... Radical concentrations peaked at methane concentration of 8–10%, but did not align with peak pressure evolution, revealing a temporal decoupling between radical activity and macroscopic overpressure.

claims.claim: The upper explosion limit (UEL) of LFP battery thermal runaway gases expands from approximately 17% to approximately 55% as SOC increases from low to high, while the lower explosion limit (LEL) remains relatively stable ranging from about 5% to 11% across all SOC levels.
claims.quote: the upper explosion limit (UEL) exhibits strong SOC dependency... UEL expanding from around 17% to approximately 55%. This widening explosive window indicates that, under high SOC conditions, once ignition occurs, the risk of complete combustion and violent deflagration significantly increases... The lower explosion limit (LEL) remains relatively stable, ranging from about 5% to 11% across all SOCs.
